items.title: Primary Workflow Complete
items.summary: The requested core path works from its initial state through a truthful completion, recovery, or explicitly bounded endpoint.
items.intent: Prevent visually polished shells from being delivered while the main user outcome remains placeholder or broken.
checks: Execute the primary path and verify every reachable control has a real outcome.
warnings: A prototype limitation must be visible and reported rather than presented as complete.
exceptions: A user requesting only a static design artifact may not require runtime behavior.

items.title: Semantic Keyboard Path
items.summary: Native semantics, programmatic names, logical focus order, and keyboard operation cover the complete core workflow.
items.intent: Ensure the interface remains operable and understandable without a pointer or visual inference alone.
checks: Navigate the primary path with keyboard only and inspect names, roles, values, and focus order.
warnings: Clickable containers and custom widgets often omit required keyboard behavior.
exceptions: Purely decorative noninteractive content has no keyboard path.

items.title: Visible and Unobscured Focus
items.summary: Every keyboard-focusable element has a visible high-contrast indicator that sticky or modal UI does not hide.
items.intent: Keep keyboard location evident throughout navigation, disclosures, dialogs, and dense control regions.
checks: Inspect focus indicators across light, dark, selected, error, and sticky-layer contexts.
warnings: Browser-default focus may become invisible after custom borders or overflow clipping.
exceptions: Noninteractive elements should not receive focus merely to satisfy this gate.

items.title: Contrast and Non-color Cues
items.summary: Text, meaningful graphics, controls, focus, and states meet relevant contrast thresholds and do not depend on color alone.
items.intent: Preserve legibility and state meaning across vision differences, displays, and environmental conditions.
checks: Measure every semantic color pairing used and inspect state cues without relying on hue.
Review hover, active, focus-visible, selected, disabled, error, and success states through source, design-state inspection, or manual rendered review when practical.
warnings: Token names do not prove the actual foreground and background pairing passes.
A foreground that passes on the default surface may fail when a state changes only the background.
exceptions: Logotypes and incidental disabled content follow the specific WCAG exceptions.

items.title: Responsive Without Loss
items.summary: Supported widths and text zoom preserve content, controls, reading order, and recovery without clipping or incoherent overlap.
items.intent: Make narrow screens and zoomed text first-class design conditions rather than a visual afterthought.
checks: Inspect at 320 CSS pixels, representative mobile and desktop widths, and 200 percent text zoom.
warnings: Hiding overflow can conceal a defect instead of fixing it.
exceptions: Inherently two-dimensional canvases and tables may use bounded scrolling with accessible controls.

items.title: Honest Content
items.summary: People, data, customer evidence, metrics, integrations, awards, and product claims are source-backed or clearly labeled as samples.
items.intent: Prevent generated UI content from misrepresenting product facts or creating false trust evidence.
checks: Trace each factual claim and evidence-like asset to a source or sample label.
warnings: Plausible placeholder content can be mistaken for real data during release.
exceptions: Clearly labeled fictional fixtures are valid in demos and automated tests.

items.title: Recoverable Errors
items.summary: Real failure paths explain what happened, preserve useful input, and provide a clear retry, correction, undo, or escape route.
items.intent: Keep users in control when validation, network, permission, or destructive actions fail.
checks: Trigger each material failure state and complete its recovery path.
warnings: Generic error toasts without context or next action do not satisfy recovery.
exceptions: A purely static page with no failure path does not need artificial error UI.

items.title: Reduced Motion Remains Functional
items.summary: Reduced-motion preference removes nonessential travel and autoplay while preserving state feedback and every user outcome.
items.intent: Avoid vestibular triggers and ensure motion is never required to understand or operate the interface.
checks: Enable reduced motion, execute the primary path, and verify feedback remains visible.
warnings: Disabling all transitions without replacement can hide state changes.
exceptions: Motion essential to the information requires a documented accessible alternative where possible.

items.title: Stable Layout
items.summary: Media, loading, validation, long labels, and async results reserve space and do not unexpectedly move controls or content.
items.intent: Prevent layout shifts, missed actions, and overlapping UI across normal state transitions.
checks: Compare geometry across loading, longest-content, error, success, and empty states.
warnings: Rigid heights can trade layout shift for clipping; use responsive constraints.
exceptions: Content regions may grow naturally when growth does not displace an active control unexpectedly.

items.title: Readable Measure and Wrap
items.summary: Headings, body copy, metadata, and actions retain intentional line lengths without brittle hard breaks or accidental one-word columns.
items.intent: Prevent responsive typography from looking cut apart when display type, side padding, or a narrow max-width consumes the available measure.
checks: Inspect the longest real heading, paragraph, label, and action immediately before and after every layout breakpoint.
Remove ordinary-copy hard breaks and reject measures that repeatedly produce one- or two-word body lines when more width is available.
warnings: A layout can avoid overflow and still be unreadable because padding or type scale leaves an unusably narrow text column.
exceptions: A short editorial phrase may use a deliberate fixed break when it remains coherent at every supported width and zoom level.

items.title: Container and Bleed Separation
items.summary: Viewport-wide backgrounds and media extend independently while text, controls, and repeated panels remain aligned to the shared content grid.
items.intent: Prevent wide-screen whitespace, double-applied gutters, and misaligned sections caused by mixing viewport calculations with constrained child panels.
checks: Compare the computed x position and width of adjacent constrained sections at wide desktop, compact desktop, tablet, and mobile sizes.
Verify full-bleed paint reaches the intended viewport edges while document scroll width remains equal to viewport width.
warnings: Using a viewport gutter formula inside an already centered max-width child applies the outside spacing twice.
exceptions: A deliberate offset or asymmetric editorial field is valid when its misalignment is specified and remains responsive.

items.title: Reference Transformation
items.summary: Studied references inform principles and craft without reproducing one source's recognizable shell, navigation, typography, and accent composition.
items.intent: Keep reference-led work specific to the target product and prevent a redesign from reading as another product with substituted copy.
checks: Name the traits retained from each reference and identify at least two structural decisions derived from the target product.
Compare thumbnail silhouettes and redesign when the shell, navigation geometry, hero structure, and accent placement still match one source as a set.
warnings: Changing colors and copy does not create a distinct system when the page silhouette and chrome remain recognizable.
exceptions: Direct reproduction is valid only when the user owns the source and explicitly requests faithful implementation.

items.title: Design-system Coherence
items.summary: Typography, spacing, color, icons, shape, surfaces, and interaction states follow one deliberate foundation.
items.intent: Prevent visual and behavioral drift caused by arbitrary values and mixed component systems.
checks: Scan the implementation for duplicate roles, arbitrary tokens, and mixed icon or component systems.
For repeated metrics, labels, icons, and controls, declare the intended center, edge, baseline, or decimal alignment and inspect rendered geometry.
warnings: Consistency should not erase necessary hierarchy or state differences.
exceptions: A migration boundary may temporarily host two systems with explicit isolation.

items.title: Authentic Primary Media
items.summary: Primary imagery reveals the actual subject users need to inspect and includes correct crop, dimensions, loading, and alternative text.
items.intent: Make media useful evidence rather than generic atmosphere or an unstable decorative layer.
checks: Inspect the subject, crop, loading behavior, reserved space, and text alternative at representative widths.
warnings: Dark overlays and aggressive crops often hide the subject and reduce contrast.
exceptions: Abstract or atmospheric imagery is valid when detailed inspection is not a user need.

items.title: Verified Delivery Report
items.summary: The final report names executed checks, inspected viewports and states, material fixes, and any remaining limitations.
items.intent: Give reviewers a factual account of confidence instead of a generic claim that quality checks passed.
checks: Separate UI/UX review, static inspection, existing project checks, rendered observations, automated evidence, and untested risk.
warnings: Do not imply that a command, viewport, browser, or e2e test was inspected when it was not.
exceptions: A conceptual-only response should state that no implementation checks apply.
